unesco_aicft_reference.title: UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Teachers
unesco_aicft_reference.note: Operational LO3.1.x–LO3.3.x indicators map to this 2024 edition (ISBN 978-92-3-100707-1); update this reference if UNESCO publishes a revised framework.

design_constraint: Domain Understanding must NOT be treated as a simple grouping of Instructional Learning Objects. A Domain represents an emergent disciplinary understanding supported by converging evidence across multiple Instructional Learning Objects, Observable Behaviours, and evidence sources. Every Domain must therefore have an explicit construct definition, evidence criteria, convergence requirements, contradiction handling rules, and theoretical rationale. Domains are assessment constructs, not curriculum topics.

terminology.domain: Emergent assessment construct synthesizing evidence across ILOs, behaviours, and sources.
terminology.not_a_domain: Curriculum topic, worksheet section, or ILO folder.

domain_pair_differentiation.discriminating_criteria: Classification evidences outcome assignment; Tree Structure evidences topology, splits, and workflow. Leaf assignment alone does not prove structural mastery.

Evaluation interprets performance artifacts; Threshold-and-Parameter Reasoning selects cutoffs and explores parameter states. Metrics may inform thresholds but do not substitute for comparative threshold decisions or documented search.
Representation is prerequisite vocabulary and structure; Generalisation requires cross-context pattern transfer beyond initial examples.

DU_DATA_REPRESENTATION.title: Supervised Data Representation Understanding
DU_DATA_REPRESENTATION.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates integrated understanding of how supervised classification problems are represented: instances as cases, features as predictors, labels as targets, and labeled datasets as the material from which decision-tree models learn. This domain emerges when vocabulary, distinctions, and structural references cohere across evidence sources — not when any single term is recalled in isolation. Integrated supervised-learning representation knowledge: instances, features, labels, datasets, and why labeled data enable learning.
DU_DATA_REPRESENTATION.theoretical_rationale: Evidence-Centered Design requires separating representational knowledge from procedural execution.

counts: Learner distinguishes features from labels or targets in at least one substantive response.
References to instances, datasets, or training role are structurally correct, not metaphorical.
Evidence spans at least two representational facets (e.g., feature + label + dataset).
Correct feature/label distinctions in worksheet or reflection with DT framing.
Dataset or instance references tied to classification tasks.
Training-role explanations linking data to pattern learning.
does_not_count: Isolated terminology recall without structural distinction (construct leakage L4).
Generic data literacy with no decision-tree or supervised-learning framing.
Writing fluency substituting for representational accuracy (construct leakage L1).
Isolated vocabulary without structural distinction.
Generic numeracy or data mentions unrelated to prediction tasks.
Polished writing without representational accuracy.
Only one representational term is recalled with no cross-facet coherence.
Feature and label are systematically conflated.
No DT or supervised-learning context in evidence.

convergence_requirements.description: At least two representational ILOs at moderate+ strength, OR one representational ILO with procedural classification corroboration from a different task.

contradiction_handling.feature_label_confusion: Cap domain at weak; block Threshold and Classification escalation.
contradiction_handling.training_role_denial: Cap at moderate; flag misconception for researcher review.
contradiction_handling.vocabulary_without_application: Treat as conceptual-only; do not infer procedural domains.

DU_DATA_REPRESENTATION.not_equivalent_to: Curriculum topic 'data vocabulary' or any single ILO folder.

confusable_with.distinction: Representation is about what is predicted and observed; classification is about executing assignment.
ILO coverage measures instructional targets; this domain requires converging representational evidence.

DU_CLASSIFICATION_REASONING.title: Classification Reasoning
DU_CLASSIFICATION_REASONING.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates executable and explainable classification reasoning: assigning class outcomes to instances using thresholds, rules, or tree paths, and articulating why a case receives a particular label. This construct emerges from coordinated procedural evidence — not from naming classification terms alone. Executable classification reasoning: assigning class outcomes via thresholds, rules, or tree paths with traceable decision logic.
DU_CLASSIFICATION_REASONING.theoretical_rationale: Classification is the central performance target of decision-tree instruction.

counts: Documented class assignment or path outcome for at least one instance or case set.
Classification act linked to a decision rule, threshold, or tree traversal — not guesswork.
Explanation references the discriminating feature or condition when justification is required.
Documented class assignments with discriminating conditions.
Rule or path traversal yielding labeled outcomes.
Threshold application producing partition or class results.
does_not_count: Label vocabulary without assignment act.
Correct answer with no traceable decision path (unverifiable inference).
Classification vocabulary in reflection only, without procedural corroboration.
Label naming without assignment act.
Reflection mentioning classification without procedural corroboration.
Correct final label with no traceable decision path.
Fewer than two procedural ILO signals at moderate+ without corroboration.
Classification vocabulary appears only in metacognitive prose (leakage L5).
Threshold misconception contradicts classification acts.

convergence_requirements.description: At least two of {classification, rule application, threshold application} at moderate+, preferably across worksheet and interactive or log evidence.

contradiction_handling.correct_label_wrong_rule: Moderate classification only; block Tree Structure strong claim.
contradiction_handling.inconsistent_class_across_sources: Apply PAT-CONTRA-001; cap at weak until resolved.
contradiction_handling.threshold_misconception_present: Block strong classification; route to misconception ontology.

DU_CLASSIFICATION_REASONING.not_equivalent_to: ILO_CLASSIFICATION or any single procedural drill.

confusable_with.distinction: Classification is outcome execution; tree structure is hierarchical topology and workflow.
Knowing labels and features does not demonstrate classification execution.

DU_TREE_STRUCTURE_REASONING.title: Decision Structure Reasoning
DU_TREE_STRUCTURE_REASONING.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates understanding of hierarchical decision structure: how nested feature tests, splits, branches, and leaves constitute a decision tree, including construction, traversal, and valid workflow sequencing. Emerges from structural evidence coherence — not from drawing a tree diagram without functional splits. Hierarchical decision-structure knowledge: nested splits, branches, leaves, and valid construction workflow ordering.
DU_TREE_STRUCTURE_REASONING.theoretical_rationale: Decision trees are structural models; procedural workflow and topological reasoning are jointly necessary.

counts: Tree construction, extension, or multi-step traversal documented with node-level decisions.
Splits reference both feature and cutoff where instruction requires node decisions.
Workflow ordering respects instructional pipeline when sequencing tasks are present.
Tree construction or extension with node-level split decisions.
Multi-step traversal following explicit if-then structure.
Correct workflow sequencing across construction stages.
does_not_count: Static diagram copying without split rationale.
Single-threshold drill presented as full tree competence.
Tool manipulation without structural change in tree topology.
Static tree diagram without functional splits.
Single-threshold drill treated as full tree competence.
Tool clicks without topology change.
Only one structural ILO is evidenced without corroboration.
Leaves or class assignments missing when structure claim is strong.
Workflow correct but topology invalid.

convergence_requirements.description: At least two of {tree structure, node split, workflow} at moderate+, OR construction with corroborated traversal on a different occasion.

contradiction_handling.valid_tree_invalid_rules: Moderate structure; block Classification strong escalation.
contradiction_handling.missing_leaves: Cap at moderate until leaf assignment evidenced.

DU_TREE_STRUCTURE_REASONING.not_equivalent_to: ILO_DECISION_TREE topic folder or worksheet section label.

confusable_with.distinction: Structure concerns model architecture; classification concerns outcome assignment.
A single node cutoff is not comparative threshold strategy.

DU_MODEL_EVALUATION.title: Model Evaluation Reasoning
DU_MODEL_EVALUATION.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates integrated evaluation literacy: interpreting confusion matrices, computing or using error metrics, comparing models, and reasoning about trade-offs among error types. Emerges from converging metric, matrix, and interpretation evidence — not from recalling a formula without application context. Integrated model evaluation literacy: matrices, metrics, comparisons, and trade-off interpretation for classifier performance.
DU_MODEL_EVALUATION.theoretical_rationale: Model evaluation connects classroom metrics to decision quality.

counts: Interprets or constructs confusion-matrix relationships among TP/TN/FP/FN.
Uses at least one performance metric (MCR, sensitivity, or equivalent) in context.
Compares alternatives or interprets feedback using evaluation evidence.
Confusion-matrix relational interpretation (TP/TN/FP/FN).
Metric computation or use in context (MCR, sensitivity).
Performance comparison or feedback interpretation.
does_not_count: Metric definition recall without numeric or matrix application.
Single-cell confusion-matrix response without relational interpretation.
Evaluation vocabulary with incorrect threshold reasoning (contradiction).
Formula recall without numeric application.
Single matrix cell without relational reading.
Evaluation vocabulary with incorrect execution.
Only one evaluation facet at moderate+ without matrix/metric complement.
Metrics contradict matrix evidence without resolution.
Reflection-only evaluation claims without artifacts.

convergence_requirements.description: At least two evaluation-family ILOs at moderate+, spanning matrix and metric or interpretation components.

contradiction_handling.metric_computation_error: Weak evaluation; do not propagate to Threshold strong claim.
contradiction_handling.excellent_reflection_wrong_metrics: Leakage L5; cap Reflective, not Evaluation escalation.

DU_MODEL_EVALUATION.not_equivalent_to: Evaluation worksheet section or isolated metric definition.

confusable_with.distinction: Evaluation interprets performance; threshold reasoning selects cutoffs using that interpretation.
Stating limitations is reflective; computing and interpreting metrics is evaluation.

DU_GENERALISATION.title: Pattern Generalisation and Transfer
DU_GENERALISATION.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates transfer of local data patterns into predictive hypotheses usable in new cases or features: citing separation patterns, selecting features on evidence, and applying insights beyond the immediate example. Emerges from cross-context coherence — partial coverage accepted given current ILO ontology gap for standalone generalisation ILO. Transfer of local data patterns into predictive hypotheses applied beyond the immediate example or feature context.
DU_GENERALISATION.theoretical_rationale: Generalisation is a core competency dimension in AI education but underrepresented in the frozen ILO set.

counts: Pattern or feature hypothesis grounded in observed data distribution or graph evidence.
Application or selection in a context different from the initial example when available.
Hypothesis linked to classification or split decision downstream.
Pattern citation grounded in observed distributions or graphs.
Feature hypothesis applied on a different task or case when available.
Downstream link to split or classification decision.
does_not_count: Generic statements about 'patterns' without feature or class reference.
Feature name recall without evidential pattern citation.
Transfer claim supported only by reflective prose (leakage L1/L5).
Generic 'pattern' language without feature/class reference.
Feature recall without evidential pattern.
Transfer claimed only in reflective prose.
No cross-context application occasion exists (cap at moderate).
Pattern hypothesis contradicted by data.
Only vocabulary-level feature knowledge is present.

convergence_requirements.description: Data-pattern or feature-selection evidence plus corroborating application on a different task or source; strong claims require explicit transfer evidence.

contradiction_handling.no_transfer_occasion: Cap at moderate; document underrepresentation risk.

DU_GENERALISATION.not_equivalent_to: A future curriculum unit or ILO folder named 'generalisation'.

confusable_with.distinction: Representation is static structure knowledge; generalisation requires cross-context predictive transfer.
Feature selection is an instructional target; generalisation is emergent transfer evidence.

DU_GENERALISATION.known_limitation: No standalone generalisation ILO in Learning_Objects; domain relies on reasoning ILOs and strategic behaviours as partial proxies.

DU_REFLECTIVE_UNDERSTANDING.title: Reflective Decision-Tree Understanding
DU_REFLECTIVE_UNDERSTANDING.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates metacognitive and critical awareness of decision-tree learning: what was understood, what remains uncertain, and what limitations apply to models or predictions. Emerges when reflection references substantive DT concepts — not when eloquence substitutes for accuracy. Metacognitive and critical awareness of decision-tree learning, limitations, and residual uncertainty with concept-specific accuracy.
DU_REFLECTIVE_UNDERSTANDING.theoretical_rationale: Reflective understanding is a distinct construct dimension in Construct_Definition.md.

counts: Reflection names specific DT concepts (threshold, tree, error type, limitation).
Acknowledges uncertainty, error, or model limits with conceptual accuracy.
Metacognitive statements tied to learning process, not generic study comments.
Reflection naming thresholds, trees, errors, or limitations accurately.
Acknowledged uncertainty tied to DT concepts.
Learning-process commentary with substantive conceptual anchors.
does_not_count: Polished prose without DT conceptual content (leakage L1).
Reflection quality used to infer Threshold or Classification mastery (leakage L5).
Prior belief recorded as mastery evidence (ILO_PRIOR_BELIEF excluded from aggregation).
Eloquent prose without DT concepts (L1).
Prior-belief guesses used as mastery evidence.
Reflection praising performance contradicted by logs.
Reflection is generic study commentary without DT anchors.
Accurate reflection is the sole evidence for procedural/strategic domains (L5).
Only ILO_PRIOR_BELIEF diagnostic is present.

convergence_requirements.description: Metacognitive or limitation ILO at moderate+ with concept-specific content; prior-belief diagnostic does not contribute to mastery aggregation.

contradiction_handling.prior_belief_only: Diagnostic baseline only; no mastery contribution.

DU_REFLECTIVE_UNDERSTANDING.not_equivalent_to: Reflection worksheet prompt or writing quality rubric dimension.

confusable_with.distinction: Fluency is a leakage source, not reflective DT understanding.
Reflective awareness is not metric computation or matrix literacy.

DU_THRESHOLD_AND_PARAMETER_REASONING.title: Threshold and Parameter Reasoning
DU_THRESHOLD_AND_PARAMETER_REASONING.construct_definition: The learner demonstrates strategic threshold-and-parameter reasoning as one integrated strategy behaviour with two complementary faces in typical CODAP or worksheet sessions: comparative cutoff judgment (setting, comparing, selecting, and justifying thresholds using classification outcomes and performance evidence) and iterative parameter search (exploring multiple parameter states through traceable tables or tool interaction and converging on a defended choice). These are not separate competences but two observable facets of the same strategic optimisation process — comparison without exploration trail yields at most moderate claims; exploration without interpreted convergence or performance linkage yields weak claims only. Emerges when evidence shows evidence-based comparison, justification, or documented search toward a reasoned parameter choice; a single correct cutoff or final answer alone is insufficient for a strong domain claim.
DU_THRESHOLD_AND_PARAMETER_REASONING.theoretical_rationale: Threshold comparison and parameter search are strategically inseparable in ProDaBi CODAP sessions; separating them risks double-counting the same optimisation behaviour.

counts: Comparison or selection among at least two threshold alternatives with stated criterion.
Justification references classification impact, error types, or performance indicators.
Threshold values are numerically or categorically explicit in evidence.
Multiple parameter states explored or tabulated with traceable changes.
Final or interim selection defended with performance or pattern evidence.
Tool or table interaction shows intentional variation, not random clicking.
Comparison among two or more threshold alternatives.
Justification citing error types, metrics, or classification impact.
Evidence-based selection at nodes or in tables.
Multiple parameter states in tables or CODAP logs.
Intentional variation with interpreted results.
Synthesis of exploration into defended final or interim choice.
does_not_count: Single threshold application without comparison or justification context.
Threshold vocabulary in reflection without performance linkage (leakage L5).
Arbitrary cutoff with no feature or outcome reference.
Single parameter value with no exploration trail.
High click volume without meaningful state change (leakage L3).
Table completion without interpretation of results.
One-off threshold application without comparison context.
Reflection praising thresholds without performance linkage.
Exploration tables without interpreted convergence.
Only a single threshold value is applied once.
Multi-source convergence is absent for a strong strategic claim.
Threshold misconception is present in evidence.
Exploration is absent or non-traceable.
Only one evidence source supports optimization claims at strong level.
Exploration does not converge toward a reasoned choice.

convergence_requirements.description: Comparative cutoff judgment and/or iterative parameter search with multi-source convergence; strong strategic claims require comparison/justification or traceable exploration with performance-linked interpretation.

contradiction_handling.threshold_misconception: Block domain escalation; counter-evidence blocks aggregation.
contradiction_handling.high_reflection_weak_threshold: Apply leakage rule L5; cap Threshold at weak/moderate.
contradiction_handling.optimization_without_interpretation: Moderate only until evaluation evidence present.
contradiction_handling.exploration_without_convergence: Weak tuning claim; moderate Threshold at most.
contradiction_handling.optimal_table_wrong_tree: Flag cross-domain contradiction; cap Tree Structure.

DU_THRESHOLD_AND_PARAMETER_REASONING.not_equivalent_to: ILO_THRESHOLD or ILO_PARAMETER_OPTIMIZATION instructional labels, worksheet item types, or a single optimised cutoff without comparison or exploration evidence.

confusable_with.distinction: Applying a cutoff is procedural; strategic threshold-and-parameter reasoning requires comparative or evaluative warrant and/or traceable search.
Evaluation interprets performance; threshold-and-parameter reasoning selects or searches parameters using that interpretation.
Tool navigation alone is not optimization competence.
